title: Odd Lots: How Rope Gave us Modern Civilization (Podcast)
title_cn: 奇怪的事情：绳索如何给我们带来现代文明（播客）
summary: Rope is easy to take for granted. It seems obvious and straightforward. But of course, it had to be invented. Early humans discovered that by twisting fibers around each other, the resulting structure would be something durable and strong. Without rope, all kinds of things aren’t possible, from lifting objects into the air to whaling or modern bridges. So how was it developed and what were the big breakthroughs in its history? summary: Dr. Martin Lemelle, Jr., Grambling State University President says he views running his HBU like an enterprise (Source: Bloomberg)

title: At Scrabble’s Biggest Tournament, Thailand Punches Above Its Weight
title_cn: 泰国在拼字游戏最大的锦标赛中表现出色
summary: Known locally as Crossword, Scrabble took off in Thailand in the 1980s after schools embraced it as a teaching aid and pathway to advancement. Much like athletic scholarships in the US, strong players can secure financial assistance and preferential admission to top universities, creating a pipeline of talent for Thailand’s competitive Scrabble circuit and helping produce a string of world champions. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Can AI Grow Without Hurting Local Communities?
title_cn: 人工智能能否在不伤害当地社区的情况下发展？
summary: The rush to build AI data centers is drawing trillions of dollars in investment and long-term bets from infrastructure firms such as DigitalBridge. But in communities where those facilities are being built, residents and officials are raising concerns about electricity costs, water use, noise, transparency and who bears the risk if demand falls short. Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel is challenging approval of a major data center project near Ann Arbor, arguing that ratepayers deserve more transparency about contracts and potential costs. DigitalBridge CEO Marc Ganzi says the industry can navigate the backlash, but only by working with local communities and showing how the benefits outweigh the burdens. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ebola Outbreak Raises Global Health Concerns
title_cn: 埃博拉疫情引发全球健康担忧
summary: Travel restrictions and border closures are hindering the response to Ebola as aid workers say they are battling to get to some of the areas where the outbreak has been the most vicious. Former Center of Disease Control and Prevention Director Dr. Tom Frieden joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hegseth Reaffirms US Commitment to Pacific Allies
title_cn: 赫格斯重申美国对太平洋盟友的承诺
summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth praised defense allies in Asia and hailed newly stable ties with China, while taking swipes at longstanding security partners in Europe. Bloomberg News Global Defense Editor Gerry Doyle joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the comments Hegseth made Saturday at an Asia security forum in Singapore, which are the latest sign of the Trump administration shifting attention toward the Indo-Pacific while expressing antagonism toward Europe and the NATO alliance. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BP’s High-Stakes Reboot Descended Into an Ugly Boardroom Drama
title_cn: 英国石油公司的高风险重启演变成一场丑陋的董事会戏剧
summary: When Albert Manifold was hired as chairman of BP Plc, he joked to friends the company needed a “gurrier” — Irish slang for a savvy and scrappy streetfighter — to help turn its fortunes around.

title: Analysis Finds Trump's Deregulation Plan Falls Flat
title_cn: 分析发现特朗普的放松管制计划落空
summary: A White House plan to rapidly repeal regulations has flopped, with just a handful of rules dumped via the expanded use of an administrative shortcut since a widespread review effort was ordered more than a year ago. Bloomberg Law Principal Legal Reporter Robert Iafolla joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ‘Dinosaur’ Tech Stocks Reborn as AI Fuels $1.7 Trillion Rally
title_cn: 人工智能推动 1.7 万亿美元涨势，“恐龙”科技股重生
summary: They were all stars of the dot-com era before fading into the background as the bubble burst and a new generation of tech darlings rose to the forefront. But Dell Technologies Inc., Nokia Oyj and Lenovo Group Ltd. are back with a vengeance thanks to the unrelenting artificial intelligence spending boom.

title: Strait of Hormuz Ship Transits Are Rising Thanks to US Help
title_cn: 在美国的帮助下，霍尔木兹海峡的船舶通行量正在增加
summary: Shipowners are increasingly optimistic about a pickup in traffic through the Strait of Hormuz after more vessels left the waterway this week with the US providing information to aid those making the journey.

title: Hegseth Praises Asian Allies, Takes Swipe at NATO
title_cn: 赫格斯赞扬亚洲盟友，猛烈抨击北约
summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth praised defense allies in Asia and hailed newly stable ties with China, while taking swipes at longstanding security partners in Europe. Bloomberg's Singapore Editor-At-Large Haslinda Amin joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the comments Hegseth made Saturday at an Asia security forum in Singapore, which are the latest sign of the Trump administration shifting attention toward the Indo-Pacific while expressing antagonism toward Europe and the NATO alliance. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Britain’s Bond Market Is Sounding the Alarm
title_cn: 为什么英国债券市场敲响了警钟
summary:
As political uncertainty grows in the United Kingdom, investors are increasingly focused on the country’s fiscal outlook and rising government borrowing costs. Bloomberg Opinion Columnist John Authers says concerns over spending, debt, and political instability are pushing gilt yields higher, reviving memories of past market crises that helped topple governments from Harold Wilson to Liz Truss. With Britain’s debt burden elevated and both major parties under pressure to spend more, the bond market is increasingly shaping the limits of economic policy. Authers argues the UK may be confronting fiscal pressures earlier than other advanced economies, offering a warning about the broader risks facing heavily indebted governments worldwide.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aluminum Under Pressure, While Poland Surges Ahead
title_cn: 铝面临压力，而波兰却一路高歌猛进
summary: It will take a long time to bring aluminum back.

title: Hanwha Aerospace Eyes Europe, US Arms Deals
title_cn: 韩华航空航天公司着眼于欧洲和美国的军火交易
summary: South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace is increasing weapons manufacturing at home, and expanding operations in Europe and the US. The company's Global Chief Strategy Officer, Alex Wong spoke with Bloomberg’s Haslinda Amin on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia’s premier defense summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hegseth Hails China Relationship at Defense Summit
title_cn: 赫格斯在防务峰会上赞扬中国关系
summary:
Jabs at European allies and talks of stable US-China ties — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth took to the stage at a security summit in Singapore this weekend. 

But two topics were notably missing from his address: Taiwan and the Iran war. Haslinda Amin with more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Pledges to Retaliate If EU Imposes Trade Restrictions
title_cn: 如果欧盟实施贸易限制，中国承诺进行报复
summary: China warned it would “resolutely” retaliate if the European Union proceeds with new restrictive trade measures, following a May 29 European Commission discussion on China policy.

title: New Zealand’s Penk on Hormuz Uncertainty (Video) (Correct)
title_cn: 新西兰彭克谈霍尔木兹海峡的不确定性（视频）（正确）
summary: New Zealand’s Defense Minister Chris Penk speaks to Bloomberg TV about how the Iran war is shaping his country’s security outlook, and the importance of freedom of navigation through the seas. He speaks with Bloomberg’s Haslinda Amin on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue. (Corrects name of interviewee.) (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Qatar Says Temporary Hormuz Fee to Clear Mines Is Negotiable
title_cn: 卡塔尔表示临时霍尔木兹扫雷费可以协商
summary: Qatar opposes permanent legal fees for transit through the Strait of Hormuz, but a temporary fee is negotiable and could help restore normal passage through the key waterway, a top official said.

title: Hanwha Aerospace Eyes Germany, UK Arms Deals as Demand Surges
title_cn: 随着需求激增，韩华航空航天公司着眼于德国和英国的军火交易
summary: Hanwha Aerospace Co. is in talks with Germany, the UK and other European nations for new weapons deals, seeking to capitalize on a massive wave of global arms spending triggered by conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East.

title: Former Ambassador Cui On Stabilizing US-China Ties
title_cn: 崔前大使谈稳定美中关系
summary: Cui Tiankai, a former Chinese ambassador to the US, explains how the US and China should work toward "constructive strategic stability." Cui, who's also a former vice minister of foreign affairs for China, speaks to Bloomberg on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: MiniMax Eyes China Listing, Takes on AI Rivals Like DeepSeek
title_cn: MiniMax 着眼于中国上市，与 DeepSeek 等人工智能竞争对手展开较量
summary: China’s MiniMax Group Inc. has begun preparations for a domestic listing, according to a regulatory filing, as the fast‑growing artificial intelligence startup challenges local rivals including DeepSeek.

title: Remembering the Life and Work of Jonathan Clements: Masters in Business
title_cn: 记住乔纳森·克莱门茨的生活和工作：商业硕士
summary: Barry sits down with Jason Zweig and William Bernstein. They discuss "Money and Me" the last book of author and journalist Jonathan Clements. Jason and William also examine Clements's approach to personal finance and impact to financial journalism. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Malaysian Defense Minister Says Won’t Rush Spending
title_cn: 马来西亚国防部长表示不会急于支出
summary: Malaysian defense chief Mohamed Khaled Nordin told Bloomberg that the country will not rush to increase its defence budget despite US pressure for partners to become more self-reliant. Khaled spoke with Haslinda Amin in an exclusive interview at Asia’s premier defense summit, the Shangri-La Dialogue. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NATO Military Head Says No ‘Drama’ With US
title_cn: 北约军事首脑表示不会与美国发生“戏剧性事件”
summary: Europe has responded to President Donald Trump’s demands to pay more for defense, a top NATO military official said Saturday, adding that the relationship with the Pentagon is stable and free of “drama.” Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, chair of NATO’s Military Committee, spoke with Haslinda Amin at the Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia’s premier defense summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How the Invention of Rope Gave Us Modern Civilization | Odd Lots
title_cn: 绳索的发明如何给我们带来现代文明零股
summary: Rope is easy to take for granted. It seems obvious and straightforward. But of course, it had to be invented. Early humans discovered that by twisting fibers around each other, the resulting structure would be something durable and strong. Without rope, all kinds of things aren't possible, from lifting objects into the air to whaling or modern bridges. So how was rope developed and what were the big breakthroughs in its history? On this episode, we speak with Tim Queeney, the author of 'Rope: How a Bundle of Twisted Fibers Became the Backbone of Civilization.' He walks us through the history of the technology, and its ongoing evolution, including how it might one day allow us to build elevators into outer space. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EasyJet Draws Early Interest From Investment Firm Castlelake
title_cn: EasyJet 吸引了投资公司 Castlelake 的早期兴趣
summary: EasyJet Plc, the UK airline that helped pioneer ultra low-cost travel in Europe over the past three decades, has drawn takeover interest from an investment firm, potentially shaking up the regional airline market as it navigates high fuel costs and steep competition.

title: Philippine Central Bank Says Inflation May Surge Further in May
title_cn: 菲律宾央行表示5月份通胀可能进一步飙升
summary: The Philippine central bank said the pace of inflation is likely to increase this month, mainly because of food-price increases and the peso’s weakness.

title: Hegseth Takes Swipe at European Defense Partners
title_cn: 赫格斯猛烈抨击欧洲防务伙伴
summary:
“Western Europe might take note.”

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth hailed stable US ties with China and praised allies in Asia, while taking swipes at longstanding partners in Europe, during a speech at a defense forum in Singapore. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hegseth Speaks at Shangri-La Dialogue  (Full)
title_cn: 赫格斯在香格里拉对话会上发言（完整）
summary: Full speech from Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth at the Shangri-La Dialogue Defense Summit, where he spoke about US-China ties, noted the importance of the Pacific region for US security, and took swipes at European members of the NATO alliance. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Legacy Tech Stocks Surge on AI Pivot
title_cn: 传统科技股因人工智能转向而飙升
summary: Bloomberg Intelligence Global Head of Technology Research Mandeep Singh joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss so-called "legacy" tech companies like Dell, Intel and Nokia seeing their stocks surge as they turn to AI. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed’s Williams Quips Economist Jobs Are Safe as AI Shift Unfolds
title_cn: 美联储威廉姆斯打趣称，随着人工智能转变的展开，经济学家的工作岗位是安全的
summary: The implications of artificial intelligence gripped global central bankers in Iceland this week, with one Federal Reserve official joking that it won’t put economists out of business.

title: AI Dangers Eclipse Nuclear Weapons at Singapore Defense Forum
title_cn: 新加坡防务论坛上人工智能的危险使核武器黯然失色
summary: The dangers of AI eclipsed those of nuclear weapons at a defense forum discussion on strategic stability in Singapore, as panelists warned it could reduce reaction times to the point where people make rash decisions.

title: Anthropic Cuts Unauthorized Platform List by Half After Pushback
title_cn: Anthropic 在抵制后将未经授权的平台列表削减了一半
summary: Anthropic PBC updated its warning about secondary markets for its shares, cutting the number of unauthorized platforms by half, after the notice caused panic among investors and a sharp rebuttal from one of the companies named.

title: Danish Pension Blacklists SpaceX Over ‘Catastrophic Governance’
title_cn: 丹麦养老金因“灾难性治理”将 SpaceX 列入黑名单
summary: A $25 billion Danish pension fund that earlier this year made headlines by ditching Treasuries as Donald Trump was threatening to seize Greenland now says it won’t touch SpaceX.

title: 25 African Startups to Watch | Bloomberg Next Africa
title_cn: 25 家值得关注的非洲初创公司 |彭博下一个非洲
summary: On this edition of Next Africa, we bring you Bloomberg's 2026 African startups to watch list. From Egypt to Mauritius, these companies are racing to solve some of the continent's toughest challenges. Plus, we hear from Moniepoint co-founder and CTO, Felix Ike, on building one of Africa's leading fintech platforms. We also speak with venture capital investors on opportunities and challenges of investing in the continent. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Social Media Giants to Pay $27 Million in School Suit Accord
title_cn: 社交媒体巨头将支付 2700 万美元购买学校套装协议
summary: The world’s biggest social media platforms agreed to pay about $27 million to settle a lawsuit filed by a rural Kentucky school district that alleged their products are addictive and helped create a teen mental health crisis that drained school resources.

title: Huge AI Bonuses in South Korea Spark Fight Over Sharing Tech Wealth
title_cn: 韩国巨额人工智能奖金引发科技财富共享之争
summary: Payouts at Samsung have raised questions over how to share the windfall from the AI boom.

title: Dell Soars Most Since 2018 on Outlook Fueled by AI Servers
title_cn: 在人工智能服务器的推动下，戴尔的 Outlook 业绩自 2018 年以来涨幅最大
summary: Dell Technologies Inc. shares surged the most since the company returned to the public markets in December 2018, lifted by an outlook for annual sales that far surpassed expectations on demand for servers that power artificial intelligence work.

title: Space Rally Gets Reality Check With Blue Origin Blowup
title_cn: 太空拉力赛通过蓝色起源爆炸获得现实检验
summary: A weeks-long rally in space-related stocks came to an abrupt halt on Friday, with the explosion of a Blue Origin rocket serving as a glaring reminder of the risks that come with the potential riches investors hope to reap from the blossoming industry.

title: Ex-Shield AI Worker Sues Over ‘Profane, Egregious’ Acts by Senior Official
title_cn: 前 Shield AI 员工因高级官员的“亵渎、恶劣”行为提起诉讼
summary: A new lawsuit accuses a senior official at Shield AI of “profane, egregious acts” of sexual harassment and alleges fraud and safety issues involving other workers at the defense tech startup.

title: OpenAI Has Discussed Adding Citigroup, JPMorgan to Bank Lineup for IPO
title_cn: OpenAI 已讨论将花旗集团、摩根大通纳入 IPO 银行阵容
summary: OpenAI has spoken with banks including Citigroup Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. about working on its upcoming initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: SpaceX Wins $4 Billion Contract for US Golden Dome Satellites
title_cn: SpaceX 赢得美国金顶卫星 40 亿美元合同
summary: SpaceX has won a contract for more than $4 billion to build satellites to track foreign aircraft and missiles as part of President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome defensive shield.

title: BOE’s Bailey Says UK Banks Still Lack Access to Mythos
title_cn: 英国央行贝利表示英国银行仍缺乏了解神话的途径
summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said that British banks are still locked out of using Anthropic PBC’s powerful new AI tool, calling for a international approach to fighting cybersecurity threats.

title: BOE's Bailey Says UK Banks Don't Have Access to Mythos
title_cn: 英国央行贝利称英国银行无权接触神话
summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey says that British banks are still locked out of using Anthropic PBC’s powerful new AI tool. Speaking with Bloomberg's Stephanie Flanders at a central banking conference in Iceland, Bailey adds that banks are using other models to test their cyberdefenses. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BOE's Bailey on AI Risks, Private Credit and Crypto
title_cn: 京东方贝利谈人工智能风险、私人信贷和加密货币
summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey discusses the emerging risks from frontier AI models, the recent concerns about the private credit market and the central bank's stablecoin proposals. He speaks with Bloomberg's Stephanie Flanders at a central banking conference in Iceland. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Lowers IPO Valuation Target | Bloomberg Tech 5/29/2026
title_cn: SpaceX 下调 IPO 估值目标彭博科技 2026 年 5 月 29 日
summary: Bloomberg’s Tim Stenovec breaks down why SpaceX is coming back down to Earth with a slightly lower valuation in its IPO. Plus, Anthropic closes a funding round at a whopping $965 billion valuation, surpassing OpenAI for the first time in the AI race; and Dell surges after the hardware giant's outlook far surpassed Wall Street estimates. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Anthropic Valuation of $965 Billion Passes OpenAI
title_cn: OpenAI 人为估值达 9650 亿美元
summary: Anthropic raised $65 billion in a funding round that valued the AI company at $965 billion including the new investment, eclipsing rival OpenAI’s value for the first time. Bloomberg's AI reporter Shirin Ghaffary joins Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Said to Lower Target for IPO Valuation to $1.8 Trillion
title_cn: 据称 SpaceX 将 IPO 估值目标下调至 1.8 万亿美元
summary: SpaceX is targeting a valuation of at least $1.8 trillion in its initial public offering, according to sources, after Bloomberg News reported in April that SpaceX was aiming for a valuation above $2 trillion. Bloomberg's space and aviation managing editor Benedikt Kammel joins Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why One of This Year’s Best Games, ‘Mina the Hollower,’ Is Only $20
title_cn: 为什么今年最好的游戏之一“Mina the Hollower”仅需 20 美元
summary: The developers at Yacht Club say that charging $20 for their slick new platformer was a no-brainer

title: Anthropic Raises at $965 Billion Valuation, Eclipsing OpenAI
title_cn: Anthropic 估值达到 9650 亿美元，超越 OpenAI
summary:
Anthropic PBC raised $65 billion in a funding round that valued the artificial intelligence company at $965 billion including the new investment, eclipsing rival OpenAI’s value for the first time.

The funding, announced Thursday, was led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks and Sequoia Capital. Each of the lead investors put in more than $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter. Sequoia declined to comment. The other three firms did not respond to a request for comment.

For more, we speak with Mandeep Singh, Global Head of Tech Research for Bloomberg Intelligence. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Investors Back Brazil’s Pax in Bet on AI-Powered Policing
title_cn: 美国投资者支持巴西的 Pax 押注人工智能驱动的警务
summary: A Brazilian artificial intelligence startup that helps police investigate violent crime is planning a rapid expansion at home, betting its technology is ready for wider use as Brazil confronts mounting public security concerns.

title: Solar Company Nextpower Hits All-Time High on AI Battery Deal
title_cn: 太阳能公司 Nextpower 人工智能电池交易创下历史新高
summary: Nextpower Inc. shares briefly touched an all-time high after the solar tracking provider agreed to buy battery company Prevalon Energy for as much as $365 million, marking the solar-tracking provider’s move into energy storage and the fast-growing AI data-center market.

title: What’s Behind the Blue Origin Rocket Explosion?
title_cn: 蓝色起源火箭爆炸背后的原因是什么？
summary: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded in a massive fireball while undergoing a test on a launchpad Thursday. Peter Elstrom explains what that means for the Bezos-backed company and its race with SpaceX. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gap Lowers Sales Outlook; Dell Soars on AI Demand | Stock Movers
title_cn: 差距降低了销售前景；戴尔因人工智能需求而飙升 |库存搬运工
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Dell (DELL) shares are soaring after the company gave an outlook for annual sales that far surpassed analysts’ estimates, fueled by demand for servers that power artificial intelligence work.
- Gap (GAP) shares plunged after the company lowered its sales outlook due to struggles with its product mix.
- NetApp (NTAP) shares jumped after the data storage provider reported its latest earnings. Analyst say the report is a strong print from the company, showing strong growth, with upgrades to consensus expected. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Autodesk CEO Says MaintainX Acquisition Presents Exciting Opportunity
title_cn: Autodesk 首席执行官表示，MaintainX 收购提供了令人兴奋的机会
summary: Autodesk President and CEO Andrew Anagnost details the company's $3.6 billion acquisition of MaintainX, emphasizing that the deal represents a strategic move to extend Autodesk's offerings from design and manufacturing into the operational phase of the built environment lifecycle. He speaks with Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Markets Are Betting Big on AI. This Harvard Professor Isn’t So Sure
title_cn: 市场对人工智能下了大注。这位哈佛大学教授不太确定
summary:
With markets near record highs, driven by demand for artificial intelligence, it certainly seems that investors are betting big on AI growth.

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, Gita Gopinath — Harvard economics professor and the former first deputy managing director of the IMF — joins Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway to discuss the possibility of a perfectly productive world driven by AI, free of social unrest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Semiconductor Stocks in 'Mother of All Supercycles,' NewEdge's Dawson Says
title_cn: NewEdge 的道森表示，半导体股票处于“所有超级周期之母”
summary: NewEdge Weath CIO Cameron Dawson discusses the outlook for semiconductor stocks during an interview with Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Colombia Lifts Ecuador Tariffs, Warns of Electoral Interference
title_cn: 哥伦比亚取消厄瓜多尔关税，警告选举干扰
summary: Colombia moved to eliminate tariffs on Ecuador’s goods on Saturday a day after Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa announced the removal of “security” levies on Colombian products.

title: Texas GOP Primary Upends Senate Race
title_cn: 德克萨斯州共和党初选颠覆参议院竞选
summary:
Attorney General Ken Paxton benefited from a late endorsement from President Donald Trump and enthusiasm from Republicans’ MAGA voters to a historic blowout victory in the Senate primary runoff over incumbent John Cornyn. Bloomberg News Texas Bureau Chief Julie Fine and The New York Times Texas Bureau Chief David Goodman joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss how hard will the Republican establishment rally behind him after backing Cornyn in a bitter campaign that burned through more than $100 million?

Many GOP leaders had argued that Paxton would be a weaker nominee because of his past legal and personal woes. But Senator Ted Cruz and Governor Greg Abbott quickly came out with statements supporting him. His opponent in November will be James Talarico, who’s been building his war chest since winning the Democratic primary outright in March. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US, UK and Australia Deepen Subsea Security Pact With Drones
title_cn: 美国、英国和澳大利亚通过无人机深化海底安全协议
summary: The Aukus alliance members — Australia, UK and the US — announced Saturday that they will develop advanced underwater drone systems and further deepen their maritime relationship that will see American submarines in Australia by next year.

title: South Korea Says It Will Hold Joint Rescue Drill With Japan
title_cn: 韩国表示将与日本举行联合救援演习
summary: South Korea says it will hold its first search and rescue drill at sea with Japan in nine years.

title: Philippines Seeks Closer Ties With Taiwan, Adversaries of China
title_cn: 菲律宾寻求与中国的对手台湾建立更密切的关系
summary: The Philippines is seeking closer ties with Taiwan and stronger military links with nations focused on deterring China’s “nefarious plans,” according to the Southeast Asian nation’s defense chief.

title: Hegseth Says US to Deter Regional Aggression With Japan, Korea
title_cn: 赫格斯称美国将阻止对日本、韩国的地区侵略
summary: The US, Japan and South Korea are “deepening our defense cooperation to ensure our combined forces remain lethal, ready, and capable of deterring aggression in the region,” Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth says in an X post.

title: Americans Hurt in Kuwait as Trump Sends Mixed Signals on War
title_cn: 特朗普在战争问题上发出混合信号，美国人在科威特受伤
summary: Several Americans were hurt in a missile attack on a Kuwaiti air base over the past day as a White House meeting to discuss extending the ceasefire with Iran ended without conclusion.

title: Zelenskyy Holds Next-Steps Talks as Kyiv, Moscow Trade Strikes
title_cn: 泽伦斯基在基辅和莫斯科贸易罢工之际举行下一步会谈
summary: Ukraine and Russia traded aerial attacks overnight, with Kyiv targeting a seaport and airbase in southern Russia, as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy held what he called a special meeting on next steps with top aides.

title: Hegseth Chides Europe, Hails Ties With China and Asia Allies
title_cn: 赫格斯谴责欧洲，赞扬与中国和亚洲盟友的关系
summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth praised defense allies in Asia and hailed newly stable ties with China, while taking swipes at longstanding security partners in Europe.

title: Hegseth Says US Developing a ‘True Friendship’ With Pakistan
title_cn: 赫格斯称美国与巴基斯坦发展“真正的友谊”
summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said “true friendship” is growing between Washington and Pakistan as Islamabad helps to negotiate an end to the war in Iran.

title: US Says Deals With Iran for Safe Hormuz Transit Are Prohibited
title_cn: 美国称禁止与伊朗达成霍尔木兹安全过境协议
summary: The US affirmed that deals with Iran to safely sail through the Strait of Hormuz — even those which don’t involve paying a toll — aren’t allowed, as the conflict between the two nations grinds on.

title: China’s Former US Envoy Says Beijing Rejects Trump’s ‘G-2’ Tag
title_cn: 中国前美国特使称北京拒绝特朗普的“G-2”标签
summary: China’s former ambassador to the US said that China’s leadership rejects any Group of Two concept between the two superpowers that’s been favored by President Donald Trump.

title: The Indian Club That Outlasted British May Not Survive Modi
title_cn: 比英国人更长久的印度俱乐部可能无法在莫迪的领导下幸存下来
summary: The Indian government is pushing to evict the Delhi Gymkhana Club, one of the country’s most storied and exclusive colonial-era social clubs, touching off a fierce fight among the city’s elite — even as it evokes a collective shrug among its young and newly wealthy.

title: US Hits Vietnam With Third Probe as Trade Pressure Grows
title_cn: 随着贸易压力增大，美国对越南进行第三次调查
summary: The US has launched a third trade investigation into Vietnam in as many months, as the Trump administration turns up the pressure on the export-reliant nation.

title: Russia Recalls Ambassador From Armenia as Tensions Continue
title_cn: 紧张局势持续，俄罗斯召回亚美尼亚大使
summary: Russia recalled its ambassador to Armenia for consultations in Moscow amid growing tensions with the South Caucasus country over its shift toward Europe.

title: Europe Is Facing Up to a Painful Vulnerability to Drone Warfare
title_cn: 欧洲正面临无人机战争的痛苦脆弱性
summary: The crash of a Russian drone into a Romanian apartment building on Friday laid bare Europe’s lack of preparedness in confronting modern aerial warfare even as fighting rages across the border in Ukraine for a fifth year.

title: Burnham Battle Gives Glimpse of Future UK Political Instability
title_cn: 伯纳姆之战让我们看到了英国未来政治不稳定的一面
summary: The most consequential parliamentary by-election in recent UK history threatens to deepen political divisions rather than fixing the country’s problems.

title: Malaysia Won’t Rush Defense Spending, Eyes Asymmetric Warfare
title_cn: 马来西亚不会急于增加国防开支，着眼于不对称战争
summary: Malaysia will not rush to increase its defense budget despite US pressure for partners to become more self-reliant, as the Southeast Asian nation seeks to balance military modernization with other critical sectors, Defense Minister Mohamed Khaled Nordin said.

title: China Expelled Reporter Over NYT Interview With Taiwan President
title_cn: 中国驱逐因《纽约时报》采访台湾总统的记者
summary: China has expelled a New York Times journalist from the country over an interview the US-based newspaper conducted with Taiwan’s president, as Beijing ramps up its campaign to isolate the self-ruled island on the world stage.

title: NATO Military Head Says No ‘Drama’ With US, Good on Spending
title_cn: 北约军事首脑表示不会与美国发生“戏剧性事件”，有利于支出
summary: Europe has responded to President Donald Trump’s demands to pay more for defense, a top NATO military official said Saturday, adding that the relationship with the Pentagon is stable and free of “drama.”

title: Americans Injured in Iranian Missile Strike on Kuwaiti Air Base
title_cn: 伊朗导弹袭击科威特空军基地造成美国人受伤
summary: An Iranian ballistic missile strike on a Kuwaiti air base within the past 24 hours caused minor injuries to several Americans and seriously damaged two MQ-9 Reaper strike drones, even as US President Donald Trump considers a deal to extend a tenuous ceasefire.

title: Trump in ‘Excellent Health,’ Took Cognitive Test, Doctor Says
title_cn: 医生称特朗普“健康状况良好”，接受了认知测试
summary: President Donald Trump “remains in excellent health,” according to the White House doctor, after undergoing a physical earlier this week which included a cognitive test.

title: China Appoints Natural Resources Minister to Lead Hubei Province
title_cn: 中国任命自然资源部部长领导湖北省
summary: China has appointed its natural resources minister, Guan Zhiou, to run the key inland province of Hubei, as part of the latest reshuffle of the country’s regional leadership.

title: NZ Should Have ‘Conversation’ on Nuclear Stance, Minister Says
title_cn: 部长表示新西兰应该就核立场进行“对话”
summary: New Zealand Defence Minister Chris Penk said the country should have a “conversation” about its long-standing anti-nuclear policy, as its ally Australia acquires nuclear-powered submarines.

title: US to Appeal Judge’s Order for Broad Refund of Trump Tariffs
title_cn: 美国将对法官要求广泛退还特朗普关税的命令提出上诉
summary: The Trump administration said it will appeal a judge’s authority to order across-the-board refunds of all tariffs ruled illegal by the US Supreme Court, potentially injecting legal chaos into a claims process that’s already underway.

title: Defense Chiefs Discuss Hormuz as Hegseth Takes Swipe at European Allies
title_cn: 赫格斯猛烈抨击欧洲盟友之际，国防部长们讨论霍尔木兹海峡

title: Trump's Reflecting Pool Renovation Tops $13M
title_cn: 特朗普倒影池翻修工程耗资 1300 万美元
summary: Bloomberg's "Balance of Power" host Kailey Leinz discusses President Trump’s renovation to one of Washington D.C’s most recognizable landmarks, the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, and the scrutiny behind how much the renovation will cost as the President wants the renovation complete before July 4th. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Holds Situation Room Meeting on Iran | Balance of Power: Late Edition 05/29/2026
title_cn: 特朗普就伊朗问题召开战情室会议权力平衡：最新版 2026 年 5 月 29 日
summary: "Balance of Power: Late Edition" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Natasha Hall, Associate Fellow at Chatham House, says the White House’s silence after President Trump was expected to decide on a new Iran cease-fire plan reflects “massive indecisiveness” over the conflict’s end goal. Rep. Yvette Clarke (D-NY) says Louisiana’s new congressional map eliminating a majority-Black district is part of a broader redistricting push that is “devastating” for Black political representation. Moody’s Analytics Chief Economist Mark Zandi says the war with Iran needs to end immediately or recession will become more likely than not. Chris Hadfield, former International Space Station Commander, suggests Blue Origin’s rocket explosion is a setback for both Blue Origin and the entire space program. (Source: Bloomberg)
